name: Sous-Vide Duck Breast, Salsify Silk, and Cherry-Infused Balsamic Glaze
description: A masterclass in precision, featuring ultra-tender duck with a glass-like crackling skin, served with an aerated salsify foam and a molecularly clarified cherry reduction.

steps.instruction: Score the duck skin in a 2mm cross-hatch pattern and season with salt and white pepper.
Place duck in a vacuum bag with thyme and garlic; use the Chamber Vacuum Sealer on 'High' to compress the meat.
Set Immersion Circulator to 135.5°F (57.5°C) and cook the duck for 90 minutes.
Prepare the Salsify Silk: Peel roots into acidulated lemon water, boil in salted water until soft, and purée with butter and cream.
Pass the purée through a Chinoise or Tamis twice to ensure zero grit.
Load the purée into the Whipping Siphon, charge with 2 N2O cartridges, and keep warm in a 50°C water bath.
Simmer cherries, balsamic, and sugar until soft; blend and strain through a fine mesh sieve to create the glaze.
Remove duck from bag and pat dry. Use the Infrared Thermometer to ensure a cold pan is at room temperature before starting.
Place duck skin-side down on low-medium heat for 12 minutes to render fat into a crispy crackling.
Flip and sear the meat side for 60 seconds; let the duck rest for 10 minutes before slicing.
Plating: Use the Siphon to create a cloud of Salsify Silk. Slice duck on a bias and fan out with tweezers.
Drizzle the cherry reduction and finish with micro-herbs and Maldon salt.

winePairings.name: Gevrey-Chambertin (Pinot Noir)
winePairings.notes: Earthiness matches the salsify while bright acidity cuts the duck fat.

winePairings.name: Vintage Port
winePairings.notes: The dark fruit notes mirror the cherry glaze perfectly.

steps.instruction: Sauté shallots in 1 tsp duck fat, add cherries and balsamic.
Reduce by 50% until syrupy, then strain through muslin cloth.
